html:
Berry Bushes are Plants commonly found in Grasslands and occasionally Forests. They can be harvested to receive 1 Berries, after which they will need from 3 to 5 Days to regrow (Rain will reduce this time). Berry Bushes can be dug up with a Shovel and replanted in a new location. Doing this will require the bush to be fertilized before it will regrow any new Berries. If the player tries to dig up a Berry Bush without fertilizing it, it will result in 2 Twigs and the bush's destruction.

Berry Bushes cannot be planted on Checkerboard Flooring, Wooden Flooring, Cobblestones, Carpeted Flooring, Rocky Turf, Sandy Turf, and Cave Rock Turf, unless it has been planted before the turf was placed.

Replanted Berry Bushes will only produce Berries for 3 to 5 harvests, after which they will require additional fertilizer. As the plant gets closer to this point, it will begin to show fewer berries in its branches. When it can no longer be harvested, it will again resemble an unfertilized bush. The time to regrow for a replanted Berry Bush increases, to a maximum of 7 days, when the Berry Bush has been harvested multiple times since it was last fertilized.

Berry Bushes are highly flammable and can be used as fuel in Campfires and Fire Pits. When burned on the ground, they will leave behind 1 Ashes. Because they burn easily, Berry Bushes planted close together are very susceptible to Lightning strikes. This can be prevented by building a Lightning Rod to draw the lightning.

Berry Bushes will not produce Berries in Winter, although any Berries left unpicked can be harvested during that time.

There are two distinct varieties of Berry Bushes, which stack separately. The "leafy" variety cannot be used to make Bush Hats. Snow will not fall on this variant. Other than this, however, they function exactly the same.

The Gobbler is a passive mob that has a 10% chance to spawn from picked Berry Bushes . It will consume Berries from bushes very quickly and can clean off multiple Bushes rapidly if not stopped.


 Downloadable Content

In the Reign of Giants and Shipwrecked DLCs, Berry Bushes will turn into Withered Berry Bushes during Summer and Dry Season. In this condition, Berry Bushes are unharvestable for their Berries and will yield 2 Twigs if dug up. A way of curing or preventing these from withering is to simply apply fertilizers or build an Ice Flingomatic nearby.

In Shipwrecked, Berry Bushes can only be found in the Jungle biome, making them rarer. However, there are islands with large Jungle areas that contain high concentrations of Berry Bushes. As only the leafy variant of Berry Bushes is available, the Bush Hat is not craftable. Berry Bushes will also spawn Snakes instead of Gobblers. Spending Dubloons at the Slot Machine has a chance to spawn a Berry Bush item and 3 Berries, making the plant renewable.

In the Hamlet DLC, Berry Bushes can be bought in Miss Sow's Floral Arrangements for 2 Oincs, making the plant renewable.

Usage

Tips

  • Given that a character with default hunger modifier needs 75 points of hunger per day, one cooked berries is 12.5 hunger, and at most berry bushes require 5 days to regrow, one may need (75 / (12.5 / 5) = 30) roughly 30 berry bushes to fully self sustain hunger off of berries alone.
  • Allowing Roasted Berries to become Rot on the ground can be a quick source of fertilizer to renew the bushes themselves. A large berry bush farm produces enough overflow to easily self sustain the fertilizing of bushes through berries rotting to be used as fertilizer.
  • Dropping a single berry in the middle of many berry bushes/a berry bush farm immediately attracts any gobblers that spawn, causing them to ignore attacks from the player allowing for easy dealing of gobblers.
  • Berry Bushes can be transplanted to grow within the Ruins, making them an excellent food source.
  • Planting Berry Bushes near one's base can make harvesting faster and less time-consuming.
  • Planting lots of Berry Bushes close by as Wickerbottom and using her book "Applied Horticulture" can produce lots of berries for the player to use in a short amount of time at the cost of sanity.
  • The size and quantity of the berries is a good indicator of how long it will be before you need to fertilize them again. If the bush has noticeably larger berries at its base, the bush has been freshly fertilized. If it looks normal, it hasn't been fertilized in a while. If it has a very low amount of berries, it will need to be fertilized once it is picked.

Trivia

  • Withered Berry Bushes share the same model as the Barren Berry Bushes.
  • Though the fruit harvested is edible, Wickerbottom states that the plant is an Ardisia crenata, aka the Christmas Berry, which (while unconfirmed) is suspected to be poisonous to pets, livestock, and/or humans.

Bugs

  • If a Gobbler jumps out of or into a barren Bush, the Bush will appear fertilized. Hovering over the Bush with Manure will display the "Fertilize" prompt. Upon reloading the world, it will become withered. These visually glitched Bushes do not grow Berries.
  • If a Gobbler jumps into a Berry Bush at dawn, the Berries will disappear because the Gobbler presumably ate them, but Berries can still be picked by hovering the mouse over them.

Assets

  • First Berrys
  • 2nd Berrys
  • Last Berrys
  • Empty
  • Barren
  • Unused Burnt
  • Leafy
  • Leafy Empty
  • Leafy Barren
  • Unused Burnt Leafy

Gallery

  • Berry Bush stages from top to bottom: dead, fertilized, unripe, and ripe.
  • Berry Bushes surrounded by Pig Torches.
  • Berry Bushes on the Map.
  • Dead bush glitch.
  • A large group of naturally spawning Berry Bushes in Shipwrecked that are already picked clean.
  • A Diseased Berry Bush in Don't Starve Together.
  • A withered Diseased Berry Bush in Don't Starve Together.
  • Berry Bush as seen in the Spring promo for Reign of Giants.
  • Berry Bush as seen in the Summer promo for Reign of Giants.
  • Berry Bush as seen in the Autumn promo for Reign of Giants.
  • Berry Bush as seen in the Winter promo for Reign of Giants.
  • Original HD icon from Bonus Materials from CD Don't Starve.
DSTDSRoGSWHam

“Red berries taste the best.”
–Wilson
“Mmmmm. Berries.”
–Willow
“Is full of food-balls!”
–Wolfgang
“A snack perchance?”
–Wendy
“PERIODIC CALORIE DISPENSOR”
–WX-78
“It's an Ardisia crenata bush.”
–Wickerbottom
“Saskastoon berries?”
–Woodie
“Reduced to eating berries. Sigh.”
–Maxwell
“I detect a slight smell of primrose.”
–Wagstaff
“A fruit bush.”
–Wigfrid
“Ripe for the pickin'.”
–Webber
“Awesome! That bush has berries!”
–Walani
“Berries!”
–Warly
“Ripe fer th' pickin'.”
–Woodlegs
“'TIS YUMMY BUSH”
–Wilba
“Full of stuff for belly”
–Wormwood
“Aww... Are all those berries for me?”
–Wheeler
“Can I eat those?”
–Winona
“Mortals say they're sweet to eat.”
–Wortox
“Snacks!”
–Wurt
“Foraging is an important wilderness skill!”
–Walter
“Aha! A ready supply of food!”
–Wanda

A ready supply of food! |choose = winona}} '''Berry Bushes''' are [[Plants]] commonly found in [[Grasslands]] and occasionally [[Forest]]s. They can be harvested to receive 1 [[Berries]], after which they will need from 3 to 5 [[Day-Night Cycle|Days]] to regrow ([[Rain]] will reduce this time). Berry Bushes can be dug up with a [[Shovel]] and replanted in a new location. Doing this will require the bush to be [[Fertilizer|fertilized]] before it will regrow any new Berries. If the player tries to dig up a Berry Bush without fertilizing it, it will result in 2 [[Twigs]] and the bush's destruction. Berry Bushes cannot be planted on [[Checkerboard Flooring]], [[Wooden Flooring]], [[Cobblestones]], [[Carpeted Flooring]], [[Rocky Turf]], [[Sandy Turf]], and [[Cave Rock Turf]], unless it has been planted before the [[turf]] was placed. Replanted Berry Bushes will only produce Berries for 3 to 5 harvests, after which they will require additional fertilizer. As the plant gets closer to this point, it will begin to show fewer berries in its branches. When it can no longer be harvested, it will again resemble an unfertilized bush. The time to regrow for a replanted Berry Bush increases, to a maximum of 7 days, when the Berry Bush has been harvested multiple times since it was last fertilized. Berry Bushes are highly flammable and can be used as fuel in [[Campfire]]s and [[Fire Pit]]s. When burned on the ground, they will leave behind 1 [[Ashes]]. Because they burn easily, Berry Bushes planted close together are very susceptible to [[Lightning]] strikes. This can be prevented by building a [[Lightning Rod]] to draw the lightning. Berry Bushes will not produce Berries in [[Winter]], although any Berries left unpicked can be harvested during that time. There are two distinct varieties of Berry Bushes, which stack separately. The "leafy" variety cannot be used to make [[Bush Hat]]s. [[Snow]] will not fall on this variant. Other than this, however, they function exactly the same. The [[Gobbler]] is a passive mob that has a 10% chance to spawn from picked Berry Bushes . It will consume Berries from bushes very quickly and can clean off multiple Bushes rapidly if not stopped. __NOTOC__ =={{pic32|Gift Icon}} Downloadable Content== In the ''[[Don't Starve: Reign of Giants|Reign of Giants]]'' and ''[[Don't Starve: Shipwrecked|Shipwrecked]]'' DLCs, Berry Bushes will turn into Withered Berry Bushes during [[Summer]] and [[Dry Season]]. In this condition, Berry Bushes are unharvestable for their Berries and will yield 2 [[Twigs]] if dug up. A way of curing or preventing these from withering is to simply apply fertilizers or build an [[Ice Flingomatic]] nearby. In ''Shipwrecked'', Berry Bushes can only be found in the [[Jungle]] biome, making them rarer. However, there are islands with large Jungle areas that contain high concentrations of Berry Bushes. As only the leafy variant of Berry Bushes is available, the Bush Hat is not craftable. Berry Bushes will also spawn [[Snake]]s instead of Gobblers. Spending [[Dubloons]] at the [[Slot Machine]] has a chance to spawn a Berry Bush item and 3 Berries, making the plant renewable. In the ''[[Don't Starve: Hamlet|Hamlet]]'' DLC, Berry Bushes can be bought in [[Miss Sow's Floral Arrangements]] for 2 [[Oinc]]s, making the plant renewable. =={{pic|32|Icon Tools}} Usage== {{Recipe |item3=Straw Hat |item2=Rope |item1=Berry Bush Item |result=Bush Hat |tool=Alchemy Engine}} =={{pic32|Prototype}} Tips== * Given that a character with default hunger modifier needs 75 points of hunger per day, one cooked berries is 12.5 hunger, and at most berry bushes require 5 days to regrow, one may need (75 / (12.5 / 5) = 30) roughly 30 berry bushes to fully self sustain hunger off of berries alone. *Allowing Roasted Berries to become Rot on the ground can be a quick source of fertilizer to renew the bushes themselves. A large berry bush farm produces enough overflow to easily self sustain the fertilizing of bushes through berries rotting to be used as fertilizer. *Dropping a single berry in the middle of many berry bushes/a berry bush farm immediately attracts any gobblers that spawn, causing them to ignore attacks from the player allowing for easy dealing of gobblers. *Berry Bushes can be transplanted to grow within the [[Ruins]], making them an excellent food source. *Planting Berry Bushes near one's base can make harvesting faster and less time-consuming. *Planting lots of Berry Bushes close by as [[Wickerbottom]] and using her [[Books|book]] "[[Applied Horticulture]]" can produce lots of berries for the player to use in a short amount of time at the cost of sanity. * The size and quantity of the berries is a good indicator of how long it will be before you need to fertilize them again. If the bush has noticeably larger berries at its base, the bush has been freshly fertilized. If it looks normal, it hasn't been fertilized in a while. If it has a very low amount of berries, it will need to be fertilized once it is picked. =={{pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia== *Withered Berry Bushes share the same model as the Barren Berry Bushes. *Though the fruit harvested is edible, [[Wickerbottom]] states that the plant is an ''[[Wikipedia:Ardisia crenata|Ardisia crenata]]'', aka the Christmas Berry, which (while unconfirmed) is suspected to be poisonous to pets, livestock, and/or humans. =={{pic|32|Mosquito}} Bugs == *If a Gobbler jumps out of or into a barren Bush, the Bush will appear fertilized. Hovering over the Bush with Manure will display the "Fertilize" prompt. Upon reloading the world, it will become withered. These visually glitched Bushes do not grow Berries. *If a Gobbler jumps into a Berry Bush at dawn, the Berries will disappear because the Gobbler presumably ate them, but Berries can still be picked by hovering the mouse over them. == {{Pic32|Research Notes}} Assets == File:Berry Bush Build.png|First Berrys Berry Bush Medium number of berries Build.png|2nd Berrys Berry Bush Small number of berries Build.png|Last Berrys Berry Bush Empty Build.png|Empty Berry Bush Barren Build.png|Barren Berry Burnt Build.png|Unused Burnt File:Berry Bush Build 2.png|Leafy File:Berry Bush Empty Build 2.png|Leafy Empty File:Berry Bush Barren Build 2.png|Leafy Barren Berry Bush Burnt Build 2.png|Unused Burnt Leafy =={{Pic|32|Blueprint}} Gallery== Berry bush.png|Berry Bush stages from top to bottom: dead, fertilized, unripe, and ripe.
